Summarize official WAF (World Armwrestling Federation) tournament rules: weight categories for senior men/women/juniors/masters, double elimination format, referee crew (head/side/chief), official fouls (slip, foul start, elbow off pad, shoulder, dangerous position, false start), warning and disqualification system, video review and protest procedure, time limits, weigh-in process, anti-doping. Cite specific WAF rulebook clauses if available.

Detail the formats and operations of premier professional arm wrestling events: East vs West (Engin Terzi), King of the Table (Devon Larratt 6ft round table no time limit), Top-8, Zloty Tur Cup, Vendetta All Stars, Pro Panja League India. For each describe: bracket format, prize purse, broadcast partners, referee crew, video review technology used, athlete contract structure, sponsor integration.

Detail the Indian arm wrestling ecosystem: Pro Panja League franchise auction season structure, Indian Armwrestling Federation IAFF, Tamil Nadu Arm Wrestling Association district structure, qualification path district to state to national to international, weight categories used in India, typical entry fees, prize money, operational pain points (manual brackets on paper, weigh-in scale disputes, slow result reporting, lack of video review, payment delays).

Compare combat sports tournament management software: Smoothcomp BJJ MMA, Trackwrestling USA Wrestling, Challonge, Toornament, Sportlyzer, BracketHQ, Tournify. List features (registration, brackets, weigh-in, live scoring, referee app, streaming integration, payment), pricing models, gaps and missing capabilities for arm wrestling needing single-table real-time scoring, video review by 3rd referee, athlete protest workflow, Indian payment rails.

Best technical architecture for a real-time sports tournament platform needing: live match scoring with sub-second latency to spectators via Supabase Realtime or websockets, video clip capture and instant replay for referee review like VAR Hawk-Eye, push-to-talk officials communication via LiveKit or Twilio, integration with YouTube Live and RTMP, Indian payment gateway Razorpay payouts UPI for prize disbursement, offline-tolerant operation at venues with poor internet using PWA and IndexedDB. Discuss tradeoffs of Next.js + Supabase + Mux + Razorpay stack.

List all stakeholder personas and workflows in an arm wrestling tournament: tournament organizer, federation admin, athlete, coach, head referee, side referee, third video referee, MC announcer, medical staff, weigh-in official, spectator, sponsor, streaming/broadcast crew, accounts payroll. For each list specific tasks before-during-after the event and what mobile/web screens they need.

Indian compliance and operational requirements for sports event prize money disbursement: TDS under section 194B for winnings from games, GST on entry fees, Razorpay Payouts/RazorpayX for bulk transfers, athlete KYC PAN Aadhaar verification, payslip generation for staff and referees, contract templates, anti-doping NADA India compliance, insurance for combat sports athletes.

Best UX patterns for live sports referee/scorekeeper apps: rapid match call (ready-set-go), one-tap fault entry, undo grace period, dual-side referee agreement workflow, instant replay scrub, conflict resolution by third referee, color-coded warnings, audio cues, large touch targets for venue use, dark mode for dim venues, single-handed operation. Examples from MMA scoring apps, BJJ Smoothcomp, cricket DRS, football VAR.
